findings.evidence: Grep hits: lines 155-162 use `FractionalIdeal.count_well_defined`, `hval`, `WithZero.log_div`, `v.intValuation_if_neg`, `WithZero.log_exp`, and `hcount` in a single `rw` chain.
findings.fix: Factor the line-160 normalization into one or two named helper lemmas, e.g. a lemma computing `-log (v.intValuation n / v.intValuation d)` as the `count_well_defined` difference, or use higher-level `FractionalIdeal.count_*` lemmas so the public bridge proof no longer relies on this long `rw` chain.
findings.issue: `fractionalIdeal_count_toPrincipalIdeal` closes the key count/valuation comparison by rewriting through representation, valuation, `intValuation_if_neg`, `WithZero.log_exp`, and `count_well_defined` normal forms, then `ring`; this is fragile against Mathlib normal-form/API changes and obscures the reusable count-difference fact.

summary: The remaining proof-quality issue is the central fractional-ideal comparison proof, which still depends on a brittle normalization chain through Mathlib internals.
